text:
(a) In this chapter:

(1) “Assembled motorcycle” means a motorcycle, as defined by Section 541.201 (Vehicles) , that is built or assembled by a hobbyist.

(2) “Assembled motor vehicle” means a motor vehicle, as defined by Section 501.002 (Definitions) (17)(A), that:

(A) has a motor, body, and frame; and

(B) is built or assembled by a hobbyist.

(3) “Assembled trailer” means a trailer, semitrailer, or travel trailer, as those terms are defined by Section 501.002 (Definitions) , that is built or assembled by a hobbyist.

(4) “Assembled vehicle” means:

(A) an assembled motor vehicle;

(B) an assembled motorcycle;

(C) an assembled trailer;

(D) a custom vehicle;

(E) a street rod;

(F) a replica; or

(G) a glider kit.

(5) “Board” means the board of the department.

(6) “Custom vehicle” and “street rod” have the meanings assigned by Section 504.501 (Classic Motor Vehicles and Travel Trailers; Custom Vehicles; Street Rods) .

(7) “Department” means the Texas Department of Motor Vehicles.

(8) “Glider kit” means a truck tractor, as defined by Section 541.201 (Vehicles) , that is built or assembled using:

(A) a kit that typically consists of a new cab, frame, and front axle and new accessories; and

(B) a used powertrain.

(9) “Hobbyist” means a person who:

(A) builds or assembles an assembled vehicle for personal use;

(B) does not engage in the continuous sale of vehicles, as defined by the department; and

(C) is not the maker of a kit or a manufacturer, as defined by Section 2301.002 (Definitions) , Occupations Code.

(10) “Master technician” means a person who holds a master technician certification issued by the National Institute for Automotive Service Excellence.

(11) “Owner” has the meaning assigned by Section 541.001 (Persons) .

(12) “Replica” means a vehicle that uses a manufactured prefabricated body or a body constructed from materials not original to the vehicle and that resembles an established make of a previous year vehicle model. The term may include a custom vehicle or street rod.

(13) “Title” and “vehicle identification number” have the meanings assigned by Section 501.002 (Definitions) .

(14) “Vehicle” has the meaning assigned by Section 502.001 (Definitions) .

(b) For purposes of Subsection (a)(4), the term “assembled vehicle” does not include a golf cart, as defined by Section 551.401 (Definition) , or an off-highway vehicle, as defined by Section 551A.001 (Definitions) , regardless of whether the vehicle is built or assembled by a hobbyist.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 731. Assembled Vehicles
##### § 731.001 — Definitions

(a) In this chapter:

    (1) “Assembled motorcycle” means a motorcycle, as defined by Section 541.201 (Vehicles) , that is built or assembled by a hobbyist.

    (2) “Assembled motor vehicle” means a motor vehicle, as defined by Section 501.002 (Definitions) (17)(A), that:

        (A) has a motor, body, and frame; and

        (B) is built or assembled by a hobbyist.

    (3) “Assembled trailer” means a trailer, semitrailer, or travel trailer, as those terms are defined by Section 501.002 (Definitions) , that is built or assembled by a hobbyist.

    (4) “Assembled vehicle” means:

        (A) an assembled motor vehicle;

        (B) an assembled motorcycle;

        (C) an assembled trailer;

        (D) a custom vehicle;

        (E) a street rod;

        (F) a replica; or

        (G) a glider kit.

    (5) “Board” means the board of the department.

    (6) “Custom vehicle” and “street rod” have the meanings assigned by Section 504.501 (Classic Motor Vehicles and Travel Trailers; Custom Vehicles; Street Rods) .

    (7) “Department” means the Texas Department of Motor Vehicles.

    (8) “Glider kit” means a truck tractor, as defined by Section 541.201 (Vehicles) , that is built or assembled using:

        (A) a kit that typically consists of a new cab, frame, and front axle and new accessories; and

        (B) a used powertrain.

    (9) “Hobbyist” means a person who:

        (A) builds or assembles an assembled vehicle for personal use;

        (B) does not engage in the continuous sale of vehicles, as defined by the department; and

        (C) is not the maker of a kit or a manufacturer, as defined by Section 2301.002 (Definitions) , Occupations Code.

    (10) “Master technician” means a person who holds a master technician certification issued by the National Institute for Automotive Service Excellence.

    (11) “Owner” has the meaning assigned by Section 541.001 (Persons) .

    (12) “Replica” means a vehicle that uses a manufactured prefabricated body or a body constructed from materials not original to the vehicle and that resembles an established make of a previous year vehicle model. The term may include a custom vehicle or street rod.

    (13) “Title” and “vehicle identification number” have the meanings assigned by Section 501.002 (Definitions) .

    (14) “Vehicle” has the meaning assigned by Section 502.001 (Definitions) .

(b) For purposes of Subsection (a)(4), the term “assembled vehicle” does not include a golf cart, as defined by Section 551.401 (Definition) , or an off-highway vehicle, as defined by Section 551A.001 (Definitions) , regardless of whether the vehicle is built or assembled by a hobbyist.

*Acts 2021, 87th Leg., R.S., Ch. 915 (H.B.3607), Sec. 20.011, eff. September 1, 2021.*
